sections.paragraph:
πολυπραγμοσύνη, ης, ἡ (s. next entry; Thu., Aristoph. et al., mostly in an unfavorable sense such as ‘inquisitiveness, officiousness, meddlesomeness’; s. esp. Plut., Mor. 515b–23) state of being overly interested in minor details, fussiness, ἡ Ἰουδαίων πολυπρ. Dg 4:6.—AAdkins, ClPh 71, ’76, 301–27.—DELG s.v. πράσσω.

Thu Thu , V b.c.—List 5

Aristoph Aristoph , V–IV b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

esp. esp. = especially

Plut Plut , I–II a.d.—List 5

Dg Dg = Letter of Diognetus, attributed to an unidentifiable apologist; II a.d.—List 1

ClPh ClPh = Classical Philology—List 6

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 847
